source: Friday Retreats Refund Metrics & Process Framework v3.2 + v2.0.1 KB anchors (critical-4 pricing-within-bounds, critical-6 defer-on-commercial-policy-scope, BV-5 errors-handled-with-grace, BV-7 gratitude-framed-private-feedback). Updated for AI envelope alignment with team envelope. Editorial sweep per drafting-discipline v2.0.1 + brand-voice v2.0.1.

aiEnvelope.principle: AI operates within the same envelope as the human team. Every refund draft is human-reviewed before send (Sprint 8-9). Every refund, regardless of amount, auto-creates a paired Ishant-Slack-notify task per corePrinciples.ceoApproval, this is a refund-specific overlay on top of the standard Mechanism A/B routing. When SP9-12 multi-dim confidence + Sprint 10+ adaptive auto-creation graduation logic ships, autoSendAutonomousTier will be populated. Until then, refunds remain fully team-gated.

handlingMechanisms.A_draft_with_mandatory_paired_notify: AI drafts proposed refund amount within severity-band recommendation. Paired Ishant-Slack-notify task auto-created. Team-review-before-send is the gate; team approves the proposed amount or revises before guest send.
handlingMechanisms.B_defer_with_paired_task: AI draft uses 'check with management' deferral language for above-envelope cases. Analyzer auto-creates paired approval-routing task per SP9-10. No commercial amount stated in guest-facing draft.
handlingMechanisms.C_ask_guest_clarification: AI asks guest to clarify when the issue or compensation request is ambiguous (e.g., affected nights unclear, scope of issue undefined).

boundsReference.refund_within_envelope: severityAssessment.severityBands. Minor: up to 5% of nightly rate per affected night. Moderate: 10-30% per affected night. Severe: 100% of affected nights' rate + relocation if needed. Standard metrics per standardRefundMetrics. Mechanism A_draft_with_mandatory_paired_notify.
boundsReference.refund_above_envelope: Above band recommendation OR above 1000 EUR absolute cost OR any legalTrigger. Mechanism B_defer_with_paired_task.

hardBlocks: Refund cost above 1000 EUR absolute (down from v1.0 1800 EUR)
Guest injury reported: stop all compensation discussions, collect evidence, no admission of liability
Legal threat from guest: cease engagement, paired LEGAL_HOLD task only
Multi-listing relocation: Ishant oversight required regardless of cost
aiEnvelope.behaviorTilt: Offer fix first (re-clean, repair, alternative) before compensation per refundDecisionProcess.step5. Do not offer refunds proactively unless clear bad-review risk per refundEligibility.proactiveRule. When a guest raises an issue privately before posting a review, lead with BV-7 gratitude-framed acknowledgment.

pairedTaskContract.refund_any_amount_within_bands: Auto-create Ishant-Slack-notify task: 'Approve refund of [X] EUR for reservation [id]. Reason: [Y]. Severity: [band]. AI proposed: [amount] per [standardRefundMetric].' AI draft proceeds with proposed amount; team-review-before-send is the gate.
pairedTaskContract.refund_above_bands_or_above_1000: Auto-create with HARD_ESCALATION flag: 'Approve refund above envelope for reservation [id]. Guest situation: [Z]. Severity: [band]. AI deferred via Mechanism B.' AI draft uses management-check deferral language, no commercial amount stated.
pairedTaskContract.refund_legal_trigger: Auto-create with LEGAL_HOLD flag and dataRetention trigger. AI draft uses hard_block_acknowledgment only. NO commercial discussion, NO admission of liability. Evidence retention per legalTriggers.dataRetention (18 months minimum).
pairedTaskContract.relocation_multi_listing: Auto-create with MULTI_LISTING_OVERSIGHT flag. AI handles communication coordination only; commercial and ops decisions route through Ishant per legalTriggers.multiListingRelocation.

autoSendAutonomousTier._status: EMPTY, populated when SP9-12 multi-dim confidence + Sprint 10+ adaptive graduation logic ships. Refunds are unlikely to graduate to auto-send autonomously given financial impact + owner-communication coupling. Most refund classes will remain team-gated indefinitely.
autoSendAutonomousTier._likely_when_populated: Possibly: minor-band cosmetic refunds <20 EUR absolute. Unlikely: moderate or severe bands. To be determined by per-class accuracy and team-edit signal.

corePrinciples.philosophy: Guest-centered, owner-fair. Refunds are only offered when guests ask, and decisions always consider all parties' interests.
corePrinciples.underpromise: Underpromise and overdeliver. Never commit to specific timeframes in guest messages without checking with the team first to ensure we can deliver.
corePrinciples.ceoApproval: Ishant (CEO) must be notified on Slack for every refund, even within team authority caps. He must be consulted before issuing any refund above authority caps. This is enforced via the aiEnvelope.pairedTaskContract.refund_any_amount_within_bands rule, every refund draft auto-creates a notify task regardless of amount.
corePrinciples.consistency: Use predefined metrics to ensure fairness and avoid subjective decisions.
corePrinciples.documentation: Every case must be logged with reason, amount, dates, and outcome in reservation notes.
corePrinciples.reviewProtection: Do everything possible to avoid negative reviews without being unreasonable financially. A 200 EUR refund can prevent roughly 1200 EUR in lost bookings over 90 days.

conditions: Guest explicitly requests compensation for an issue
The issue is valid, verified, and occurred during the stay
It would help maintain goodwill, prevent bad reviews, or is fair given the situation
The impact was not caused by the guest
refundEligibility.proactiveRule: Do not offer refunds proactively unless there is a clear risk of a bad review.

severityAssessment.description: Evaluate severity using five qualitative dimensions to determine the appropriate compensation band. Do NOT assign numeric scores, use the dimensions as reasoning criteria.

dimensions.name: Essentiality
dimensions.description: How essential was the affected feature to the stay?
dimensions.range: Cosmetic issue -> Core amenity failure -> Property uninhabitable

dimensions.name: Duration
dimensions.description: How long did the issue persist?
dimensions.range: Under 1 hour -> Several hours -> Multiple days -> Entire stay

dimensions.name: Guest Impact
dimensions.description: How many guests were affected and how severely?
dimensions.range: Single guest minor inconvenience -> Full party significantly disrupted -> Guests forced to leave

dimensions.name: Timing
dimensions.range: Daytime (easier to resolve) -> Night-time or arrival/departure (harder to resolve, higher stress)

dimensions.name: Responsibility
dimensions.range: Guest caused -> External/force majeure -> Shared responsibility -> Clearly host/property fault

minor.description: Cosmetic or brief issues with low guest impact, often guest-caused or external
minor.compensationRange: Up to 5% of nightly rate per affected night

moderate.description: Core amenity issues lasting several hours affecting guest comfort, host responsibility likely
moderate.compensationRange: 10-30% of nightly rate per affected night
examples: Broken A/C for several hours in summer
No hot water for a day

severe.description: Essential services failed for extended periods, full party significantly impacted, clearly host fault
severe.compensationRange: 100% of affected nights' rate + relocation if needed
severe.mechanism: A_draft_with_mandatory_paired_notify if absolute cost <=1000 EUR, otherwise B_defer_with_paired_task
examples: Full night lost due to check-in failure

standardRefundMetrics.situation: Issue impacting stay (e.g. broken A/C, no hot water)
standardRefundMetrics.recommendation: 30% of nightly rate per affected night
standardRefundMetrics.notes: Per Airbnb's recommendation. Assess severity dimensions first.

standardRefundMetrics.situation: Full night lost (e.g. check-in issue, severe issue)
standardRefundMetrics.recommendation: 100% of that night's rate (excluding cleaning fee)
standardRefundMetrics.notes: Only if guest genuinely couldn't stay.

standardRefundMetrics.recommendation: Free re-cleaning + optional 10-30% refund for 1 night
standardRefundMetrics.notes: Depends on severity and guest reaction. Always offer re-cleaning first per refundDecisionProcess.step5.
Use judgment depending on importance of the amenity.
Based on level of impact. Construction, blocked access, etc.

refundDecisionProcess.detail: Only proceed if guest explicitly asks for compensation. Don't offer proactively unless there is a clear risk of a bad review.
What happened? How many nights were affected? Assess against the five severity dimensions.
Use the compensation percentages from the severity bands. Base calculation on actual nightly rate after discount (excluding cleaning fee).
Was this caused by owner negligence, guest negligence, or a one-off event? Avoid refunds for issues outside our control unless severe.
If the issue can be resolved (re-clean, repair, alternative), always offer a solution before compensation. AI draft should lead with the fix offer; if guest accepts fix, refund may not be needed.

Within severity band recommendation AND total absolute cost <=1000 EUR: Mechanism A_draft_with_mandatory_paired_notify (AI drafts proposed amount, paired Ishant-Slack-notify task auto-created). Above band OR above 1000 EUR OR legal trigger: Mechanism B_defer_with_paired_task (deferral language, no commercial amount stated, paired approval task).
Add note to guest reservation with: incident summary, cost breakdown, and outcome. Notify owner with ROI argument if relevant per ownerCommunication.requirement.

communicationFramework.name: 4E Model

steps.description: Acknowledge the guest's frustration genuinely. Show you understand the impact.
Briefly explain what happened and what you're doing about it. Be transparent without over-sharing.
Take concrete action, fix the issue, offer compensation, or arrange an alternative.
Turn the negative into a positive memory. Follow up to confirm resolution and satisfaction.

communicationFramework.brandVoiceCrossReference: BV-5 errors-handled-with-grace governs the Empathise step. BV-7 gratitude-framed-private-feedback applies when the guest raises the issue privately before posting a review. BV-8 close-hospitality-comms-with-warmth applies to the Elevate step closing.

relocationProtocol.name: R-3-60
relocationProtocol.description: Recognise within 5 minutes -> React within 30 minutes with alternative + transport -> Resolve within 60 minutes
costHierarchy: Equal Friday unit (best, no extra cost)
Friday upgrade (builds goodwill at minimal cost)
OTA relocation (absolute last resort, platform may invoice host)
fullRefundTriggers: Double booking with no alternative found within 60 minutes
relocationProtocol.mechanism: B_defer_with_paired_task for any multi-listing relocation per legalTriggers.multiListingRelocation. Single-property relocation within Friday inventory: A_draft_with_mandatory_paired_notify.

slaTargets.serviceRecoveryParadox: +12% chance of guest leaving 5 stars after quick, effective fix

airbnb.cancellation: 24-hour free cancellation for bookings made >=7 days before check-in
airbnb.issueReporting: Guest must report 'Reservation Issue' within 72 hours
airbnb.serviceFeeRule: Do not refund Airbnb's service fee unless platform requires it

bookingCom.alternativeOffer: Host must offer comparable alternative within 30 minutes of complaint
bookingCom.failureConsequence: Booking.com relocates guest and invoices host
bookingCom.lowReliabilityFlag: Complaint invoice >250 EUR triggers 'Low Reliability' flag on profile

ownerCommunication.requirement: Every owner notification must include: incident summary, cost breakdown, ROI argument

costAssignment.ownerFault: Assign to owner ledger under 'Guest Recovery, Owner Fault'
costAssignment.sharedResponsibility: Split or absorb depending on relationship and circumstance

ownerCommunication.aiScope: AI does not draft owner-facing communication in this Sprint. Owner notify is a team-side action via paired task per ownerCommunication.requirement. AI's role is limited to guest-facing draft and the paired team-notify task; owner messaging is downstream.

legalTriggers.guestInjury: Stop all compensation discussions. Collect photos and evidence. Notify management immediately. Complete insurer form PL-78934. Do NOT admit liability. AI uses hard_block_acknowledgment only.
legalTriggers.legalThreat: Escalate to management immediately. Do not engage further without direction. AI uses hard_block_acknowledgment only.
legalTriggers.costAboveThreshold: Any cost above 1000 EUR must be escalated to management before action. Down from v1.0 1800 EUR.
legalTriggers.multiListingRelocation: Management oversight required for any relocation involving multiple properties. AI handles communication coordination only.
legalTriggers.dataRetention: Retain all evidence for at least 18 months.

macroLibrary.description: Apology for minor issue with fix in progress
macroLibrary.usage: Minor severity issues where fix is underway
macroLibrary._template_authoring_status: DEFERRED, templates currently live in Guesty saved replies / Helpscout, not in this KB. Corpus-paired template authoring deferred to follow-on session (similar to brand-voice Phase 5 corpus pairing pattern).

macroLibrary.description: Apology for issue with fix pending/scheduled
macroLibrary.usage: Issues requiring scheduled maintenance or next-day fix

macroLibrary.description: Cleaning fee refund offer for dirty check-in
macroLibrary.usage: Dirty apartment on arrival, offer re-clean plus partial refund
Extended WiFi outage affecting remote workers or connectivity-dependent guests
Important amenity missing or broken, moderate inconvenience

macroLibrary.description: Relocation offer to equal Friday property
macroLibrary.usage: Severe issue requiring relocation, equal unit available
Severe issue requiring relocation, upgrade available (builds goodwill)
Moderate issues where future credit is more cost-effective than immediate refund
Property genuinely unsafe or uninhabitable, full night refund required
After any refund, owner must be informed with cost breakdown and ROI argument
macroLibrary.mechanism: OWNER_SCOPE, not in AI draft scope this Sprint

macroLibrary.usage: After relocation, owner informed of reason, cost, and prevention steps
macroLibrary.mechanism: OWNER_SCOPE, not in AI draft scope this Sprint

macroLibrary.description: Request for positive review after issue resolution
macroLibrary.usage: After successful service recovery, request review to offset any negative impact
macroLibrary.mechanism: A_autonomous_draft (no mandatory notify, this is post-recovery follow-up not a refund)

aboveEnvelopeMacros._description: Canonized Mechanism B phrasings for above-envelope refund cases. Pair each with auto-created team-approval task per aiEnvelope.pairedTaskContract.
aboveEnvelopeMacros.refund_above_bands: I'd like to discuss this with our management team to see what we can do. I'll come back to you with their decision shortly.
aboveEnvelopeMacros.refund_above_1000_cost: I'd like to discuss this with our management team. They handle decisions of this size directly, and they'll come back to you shortly.
aboveEnvelopeMacros.refund_legal_trigger: Thank you for letting us know. I'll bring this to our management team's attention and they'll be in touch with you directly.
aboveEnvelopeMacros.hard_block_acknowledgment: Thank you for sharing this with us. Our management team is reviewing the situation and will be in direct contact with you shortly.
aboveEnvelopeMacros.multi_listing_relocation: We're working on a solution for you across our properties. I'd like to coordinate with our management team to make sure we get this right. They'll come back to you shortly.

economicsContext.reviewImpact: One 3-star review causes approximately -13% drop in bookings for 90 days
economicsContext.ratingValue: Every +0.1 star adds approximately 4% more future bookings
economicsContext.serviceRecoveryParadox: +12% chance of 5-star review after effective quick fix vs. problem-free stay
economicsContext.futureStayCreditRedemption: 75% never redeemed, 22% of recipients book again

changes: Added aiEnvelope top-level block: AI = team envelope principle, handlingMechanisms with new A_draft_with_mandatory_paired_notify variant (refund-specific overlay on top of A/B), pairedTaskContract spec for refund/legal/multi-listing routing, autoSendAutonomousTier slot reserved for Sprint 10+ (likely stays empty for refunds given financial impact)
severityBands re-tagged with mechanism field. Minor and moderate: always A_with_paired_notify. Severe: A if <=1000 EUR, else B
standardRefundMetrics re-tagged with mechanism per situation
Cost above threshold tightened: 1800 EUR -> 1000 EUR (legalTriggers.costAboveThreshold)
refundDecisionProcess.step6 renamed 'Determine Envelope and Mechanism', expanded with explicit A vs B routing
communicationFramework.brandVoiceCrossReference added: BV-5, BV-7, BV-8 anchored to 4E steps
relocationProtocol.mechanism field added: multi-listing always B, single-property within Friday inventory is A_with_paired_notify
ownerCommunication.aiScope clarification: AI does not draft owner-facing this Sprint; owner notify is downstream paired-task team action
legalTriggers: 'Notify Ishant' generalized to 'Notify management' in canonized action wording per OP-4 (Ishant retained in corePrinciples documentation context)

macroLibrary: each macro tagged with mechanism. _template_authoring_status: DEFERRED added to all 12 macros, actual template text not in this KB, lives in Guesty saved replies / Helpscout. Corpus-paired template authoring deferred to follow-on session (same pattern as brand-voice Phase 5)
owner_notify_refund and owner_notify_reloc macros tagged OWNER_SCOPE (not in AI draft scope this Sprint)
review_request_positive macro: A_autonomous_draft no mandatory notify (post-recovery follow-up, not a refund)
New aboveEnvelopeMacros section: 5 canonized Mechanism B phrasings (refund_above_bands, refund_above_1000_cost, refund_legal_trigger, hard_block_acknowledgment, multi_listing_relocation)
Initial version, adapted from Friday Retreats Refund Metrics & Process Framework v3.2 for current team structure
